Dolittle (2020)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $77,047,065Details
International Box Office $174,462,229
Worldwide Box Office $251,509,294
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,269,560
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $10,798,667
Total $19,068,227
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After losing his wife seven years earlier, the eccentric Dr. John Dolittle, famed doctor and veterinarian of Queen Victoria’s England, hermits himself away behind the high walls of Dolittle Manor with only his menagerie of exotic animals for company. But when the young queen falls gravely ill, a reluctant Dolittle is forced to set sail on an epic adventure to a mythical island in search of a cure, regaining his wit and courage as he crosses old adversaries and discovers wondrous creatures.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$21,844,045 (28.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:30.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$175,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,155 opening theaters/4,155 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $100,379,395

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 17th, 2020 (Wide) by Universal
January 17th, 2020 (IMAX) by Universal
International Releases: January 8th, 2020 (Wide) (South Korea)
January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (Ukraine)
January 10th, 2020 (Wide) (South Africa)
January 15th, 2020 (Wide) (Trinidad)
Video Release: March 24th, 2020 by Universal Home Entertainment
April 7th, 2020 by Universal Pictures UK
MPA Rating: PG for some action, rude humor and brief language.
(Rating bulletin 2607 (Cert #52492), 12/4/2019)
Running Time: 103 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Remake, Scene in End Credits
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Talking Animals, Veterinarian
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Family Movie
Time period setting: 1800s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Team Downey, Universal Pictures, Perfect World Pictures, Roth Kirschenbaum Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After losing his wife seven years earlier, the eccentric Dr. John Dolittle, famed doctor and veterinarian of Queen Victoria’s England, hermits himself away behind the high walls of Dolittle Manor with only his menagerie of exotic animals for company. But when the young queen falls gravely ill, a reluctant Dolittle is forced to set sail on an epic adventure to a mythical island in search of a cure, regaining his wit and courage as he crosses old adversaries and discovers wondrous creatures.
